summary: Descriptive overview of contractformation in substantive law: offer, acceptance, consideration, mutual assent.
key_points: offer is a fundamental concept in contract law.
Legal standards for contractformation are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine contractformation in litigation.
The doctrine of contractformation has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding contractformation requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
